mission.title: Install and Configure kubeadm (Kubernetes bootstrapper)
mission.description: Kubeadm is a tool designed to simplify the process of creating and managing Kubernetes clusters. It provides best-practice paths for bootstrapping a secure cluster and is ideal for users who want a straightforward setup without extensive configuration. However, it requires a certain level of familiarity with Kubernetes concepts and may not be suitable for those seeking a fully managed solution.

steps.title: Install Kubeadm
steps.description:
This step installs the kubeadm binary on your system, which is necessary for initializing and managing your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -s https://packages.cloud.google.com/apt/doc/apt-key.gpg | sudo apt-key add -

echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list

sudo apt-get update

sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

```

> Add the Google Cloud public key for package verification.
> Add the Kubernetes APT repository.
> Update the package list to include the new repository.
> Install specific versions of kubeadm, kubelet, and kubectl.
commands: echo 'deb https://apt.kubernetes.io/ kubernetes-xenial main' | sudo tee /etc/apt/sources.list.d/kubernetes.list
sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

steps.title: Initialize the Kubernetes Control Plane
steps.description:
This command initializes the control plane of your Kubernetes cluster, setting up the necessary components.

```bash

sudo kubeadm init --kubernetes-version v1.31.0 --pod-network-cidr=192.168.0.0/16

```

> Initialize the cluster with a specific Kubernetes version and pod network CIDR.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to communicate with your new cluster.

```bash

mkdir -p $HOME/.kube

sudo cp -i /etc/kubernetes/admin.conf $HOME/.kube/config

sudo chown $(id -u):$(id -g) $HOME/.kube/config

```

> Create the kube directory in your home.
> Copy the admin config file to your kube directory.
> Change ownership of the kube config file.

steps.title: Install a Pod Network Add-on
steps.description:
This step installs a network add-on to allow communication between pods in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://docs.projectcalico.org/manifests/calico.yaml

```

> Apply the Calico network add-on.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your cluster to ensure everything is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> List all nodes and their statuses.

uninstall.title: Reset the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command will remove all Kubernetes components and configurations from the node. Data loss may occur, and orphaned resources may remain.

```bash

sudo kubeadm reset

sudo rm -rf $HOME/.kube

```

> Reset the kubeadm setup.
> Remove the kube config directory.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster state. This process will upgrade your cluster to a newer version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

sudo kubeadm upgrade plan

sudo kubeadm upgrade apply v1.31.0

kubectl uncordon <node-name>

kubectl get nodes

```

> Drain the node to prepare for upgrade.
> Check available upgrades.
> Apply the upgrade to the specified version.
> Uncordon the node after upgrade.
> Verify the nodes are updated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubelet not running'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubelet service may not be started or is failing to run.

**Fix:**
Run 'sudo systemctl start kubelet' to start the kubelet service.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'kubeadm init failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This could be due to network issues or insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the system requirements and ensure the network is properly configured.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The kubeconfig file may not be set up correctly or the API server is down.

**Fix:**
Ensure the kubeconfig file is correctly configured and the API server is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'pod network not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The network add-on may not be installed or configured correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the network add-on and verify its configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new features such as the PodSecurity admission controller and improved handling of network policies.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of --pod-network-cidr flag in favor of more specific network configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.30, ensure to review the new network policy configurations.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with the status 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get nodes'.

sudo apt-get install -y kubeadm=1.31.0-00 kubelet=1.31.0-00 kubectl=1.31.0-00

prerequisites.description: Ensure your system meets the minimum resource requirements and has the necessary tools installed.
